_disclaimer: SPECULATIVE forward conviction-research over a LIVE-DISCOVERED small/mid-cap universe. NOT a backtested edge; thin/no clean history; small-caps carry liquidity, fraud, and total-loss risk. Research leads, you decide.
_method_note: Sweep surfaced ~14 distinct candidates across 6 angles. Anti-hype guard DROPPED names already up >150% over 1Y or already consensus/well-covered: INTT (+172% 1Y, only-cheap-after-the-run), IPGP (+78% 1Y but 27 analysts / $5B = not early), ONDS (+487% 1Y, $4.8B = fully discovered), AMBQ (+217% YTD, trading at 98% of 52w range, deep losses + giant EPS miss = froth), RFIL (+266% 1Y, source itself flagged retail mania). Kept 7 genuinely earlier names below.

discoveries.name: Fortrea Holdings Inc.
discoveries.valuation: trades on turnaround EPS ramp; FY26 EPS est ~0.79 building to ~1.30+ by FY28 off a ~$15 stock
discoveries.thesis: Post-spin CRO that the Street left for dead (consensus still only Hold, 1 sell) but the operational turnaround is inflecting: Q1 2026 EPS beat by +433% (0.16 vs 0.03 est) and forward EPS estimates step up every quarter through 2028. Citi explicitly flagged Fortrea as a low-expectations name now benefiting from rising earnings revisions. Classic 'cheap + quietly improving + still-doubted' early setup before coverage warms up.
discoveries.key_risk: Highly leveraged (net debt large vs EBITDA), EBITDA still modeled negative near-term, beta ~2.1; a single bookings/biotech-funding miss could reset the turnaround narrative. Revenue growth is only low-single-digit, so the whole thesis rests on margin/EPS recovery, not top-line.

news.headline: Citi favors select small value stocks showing improving fundamentals, highlighting Fortrea Holdings benefiting from rising earnings expectations after low investor expectations
Fortrea Q1 2026 EPS 0.16 vs 0.03 estimate (+433% surprise), revenue $636.5M topping estimates

discoveries.name: Agilysys, Inc.
discoveries.valuation: premium SaaS multiple, but FY EPS ramping ~0.40 (Jun-26) toward ~1.4-1.6 by 2028-29
discoveries.thesis: Hospitality-software (POS/PMS) compounder that fell -27% YTD and sits only 31% of its 52w range (now $87 vs $145 high) while fundamentals keep improving: 8 unanimous Buys, latest quarter beat EPS by +27%, and forward estimates rise every period. A high-quality recurring-revenue business that has 'based out' after a sharp de-rate is exactly the early re-rating setup before the crowd returns.
discoveries.key_risk: Not statistically cheap (SaaS valuation), so re-rating depends on sustained beats; hospitality IT spend is discretionary and cyclical. The unanimous-Buy book means it is already discovered by specialists — the edge is the price drawdown, not obscurity.
early_signals: unanimous analyst Buys but price still depressed

news.headline: Wasatch Small Cap Growth Strategy believes Agilysys (AGYS) defies market's fears
Agilysys fiscal Q4 EPS 0.63 vs 0.50 estimate (+27% surprise), revenue $82.9M beat

discoveries.name: GigaCloud Technology Inc.
discoveries.valuation: ~9x fwd P/E (FY26 EPS est ~3.7-4.0 vs $34 price)
discoveries.thesis: Profitable B2B cross-border e-commerce marketplace for bulky goods, genuinely under-covered (only 3 analysts) and cheap at roughly 9x forward earnings after a -13% YTD pullback to mid-range. Q1 beat EPS by +20% with consistent profitability — a real, cash-generating small-cap that screens cheap-and-improving rather than a story stock.
discoveries.key_risk: China-domiciled (Cayman holdco, Suzhou HQ) = VIE/geopolitical/tariff and audit-trust overhang that caps the multiple; bulky-goods demand is tied to US housing/discretionary cycles. Thin coverage cuts both ways (illiquid, volatile).

news.headline: GigaCloud Technology highlighted as a standout small-cap performer powered by consistent earnings beats and its end-to-end cross-border B2B supply-chain platform
GigaCloud Q1 2026 EPS 1.04 vs 0.87 estimate (+20% surprise), revenue $359.5M beat

discoveries.name: TAT Technologies Ltd.
discoveries.valuation: rich on trailing EV/EBITDA but EPS scaling fast; FY EPS est ramping ~1.3 (2026) toward ~2.2+ (2027)
discoveries.thesis: Israeli commercial/military aerospace MRO and heat-transfer OEM riding the global aftermarket up-cycle: 5 unanimous Buys (price target ~$56 vs $46), Q1 beat EPS by +37%, and forward EPS/revenue estimates step up sharply through 2027 as MRO backlog converts. Under-followed defense/aero name with a clear earnings-acceleration story.
discoveries.key_risk: Already +82% over the past year (getting late vs a true 'pre-run' entry, though under the 150% guard); Israel-domiciled (geopolitical risk), small float and long cash-conversion cycle (heavy inventory). Valuation needs the EPS ramp to materialize on schedule.

news.headline: TAT Technologies posts upbeat Q1 earnings, shares jump 11.7%
TAT Technologies Q1 2026 EPS 0.26 vs 0.19 estimate (+37% surprise), revenue $41.1M beat

discoveries.name: M-tron Industries, Inc.
discoveries.valuation: ~37x trailing EPS but profitable & debt-light; FY EPS est ~2.5 (2026)
discoveries.thesis: Tiny ($291M) profitable RF/microwave frequency-control supplier to aerospace/defense/space, covered by only ONE analyst — about as under-the-radar as a real, cash-generating company gets. Net-cash balance sheet (current ratio ~12x), Zacks Bull-of-the-Day, beat Q1 by +10% with rising estimates and defense-electronics backlog tailwinds.
discoveries.key_risk: Already +142% over the past year and trading at ~91% of its 52w range — the easy 'early' money may be gone and a pullback is plausible; single-analyst coverage = extreme illiquidity/volatility, and one defense program slip moves the whole P&L.

news.headline: M-tron (MPTI) highlighted as Zacks Bull of the Day
M-tron Q1 2026 EPS 0.67 vs 0.61 estimate (+10% surprise), revenue $14.7M beat

discoveries.name: BigBear.ai Holdings, Inc.
discoveries.valuation: EV on ~5x sales of a money-losing name; no P/E (negative EPS)
discoveries.thesis: Defense-AI decision-support name that has based out near the bottom of its range ($3.92, only 14% of 52w range, -27% YTD) while its contract backlog grew +14% q/q to ~$282M including a $53M classified sole-source military award. If Pentagon AI spend accelerates and losses narrow, the low base + growing backlog is the lottery-style early-mover setup the brief is after.
discoveries.key_risk: Unprofitable with EPS estimates still deeply negative (no path to GAAP profit in the model window), Hold consensus, beta ~3.1 and frequent dilution — this is a high-volatility, backlog-on-faith story that can keep grinding lower or dilute holders. Revenue base is still tiny relative to the $1.4B market cap.

news.headline: Under-the-radar defense-tech small caps: BigBear.ai backlog grew 14% q/q to $281.9M incl. $53M classified sole-source military award
BigBear.ai Q1 2026 revenue $34.4M beat estimate (+2.5%); EPS -0.08 in line

discoveries.name: Palladyne AI Corp.
discoveries.valuation: pre-scale; no meaningful P/E (negative EPS), <$10M revenue
discoveries.thesis: Micro-cap ($272M) AI-autonomy software for third-party robots and Class-1 UAVs (defense/industrial), essentially pre-revenue with only 1 analyst — a true under-the-radar optionality play on robot/drone autonomy. Off its lows but still 32% of range; if Palladyne IQ/Pilot land design wins, the tiny base offers asymmetric upside.
discoveries.key_risk: Weakest fundamentals in this list: Q1 revenue MISSED by -28% and EPS missed by -70%, revenue is under $10M against a $272M cap, cash burn is heavy and beta ~3.6. This is venture-stage risk in public markets — real total-loss / dilution potential; included only as a high-variance lottery ticket, not a fundamentals pick.

news.headline: Palladyne AI cited among small-cap robotics/defense-AI names; Palladyne Pilot targets Class-1 UAV multi-drone situational awareness
Palladyne AI Q1 2026 revenue $3.5M missed estimate (-28%); EPS -0.28 missed (-70%)
